summary: A podcast directory listing for The Iris Murdoch Society podcast. While primarily containing metadata, episode descriptions, and guest lists, it includes brief scholarly summaries of Murdoch's moral philosophy, specifically her views on the nature of truth and realism.

principles.statement: Truth is a personal, ethically infused, and hierarchically nuanced concept.
principles.rationale: Viewing truth through an ethical lens shifts our understanding of realism, demonstrating that moral perception directly informs our understanding of the world.
principles.source_quote: Murdoch treats truth as a personal, ethically infused, and hierarchically nuanced concept rather than a binary, impersonal property of propositions

anti_patterns.description: Treating truth merely as a binary, impersonal property of propositions.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It fails to account for how moral perception informs our understanding of the world and ignores the personal, ethically infused nature of reality.
